name: Vic – Gurb - Santa Eulàlia de Riuprimer – La Guixa – Vic Route
name_original: Ruta BTT official de Ossona 3 - circular
description: This difficult 12.8-mile mountain biking route near Vic offers varied terrain, passing through towns and forests with 705 feet of gain.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Vic features a diverse landscape characterized by rolling hills, forested areas, and open woodlands. The region offers a variety of terrain, including both challenging climbs and flowing descents. Riders can expect paths that traverse agricultural lands and natural areas, providing varied surfaces for mountain biking. This area provides opportunities for exploring both established trails and more natural paths. Best mountain bike trails around Vic The most popular mountain bike trail is Vic – Gurb - Santa Eulàlia de Riuprimer – La Guixa – Vic Route, a 12.8 miles (20.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 31 minutes to complete. This route offers a challenging ride through varied terrain with significant elevation changes. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Creu de la Miranda – Ermita de Sant Sebastià loop from Vic, a difficult 18.4 miles (29.6 km) path. This trail features sustained climbs and descents, winding through natural landscapes with scenic viewpoints. Local mountain bikers also love the Sant Quirze de Muntanyola – Las Jondras Trail loop from Vic, a 20.2 miles (32.6 km) trail leading through forested sections and open countryside, often completed in about 2 hours 59 minutes. Mountain biking around Vic is defined by rolling hills, agricultural landscapes, and mixed woodlands. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate cross-country rides to more difficult, technical trails. The routes in Vic are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 130 reviews. More than 2900 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Vic's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Vic?
A: There are over 125 mountain bike trails around Vic, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a good mix of easy, moderate, and difficult routes to explore.

Q: What is the general terrain like for mountain biking in Vic?
A: Mountain biking around Vic features a diverse landscape characterized by rolling hills, forested areas, and open woodlands. Riders can expect paths that traverse agricultural lands and natural areas, providing varied surfaces for mountain biking, including both challenging climbs and flowing descents.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders?
A: Yes, Vic offers a significant number of challenging trails. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3983480" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Creu de la Miranda – Ermita de Sant Sebastià loop from Vic&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 18.4-mile (29.6 km) path featuring sustained climbs and descents through natural landscapes with scenic viewpoints. There are 66 difficult routes in total.

Q: Are there any moderate mountain bike trails suitable for intermediate riders?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32101625" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Quirze de Muntanyola – Las Jondras Trail loop from Vic&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate 20.2-mile (32.6 km) trail that leads through forested sections and open countryside. There are 44 moderate routes available.

Q: What kind of scenic viewpoints can I expect on the trails around Vic?
A: Many trails offer scenic viewpoints. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3983480" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Creu de la Miranda – Ermita de Sant Sebastià loop from Vic&lt;/a&gt; provides stunning vistas. You can also visit specific viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1381354" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Roc Gros Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5074387" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gurb Cross&lt;/a&gt;, which are accessible from some routes.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Vic?
A: The mountain biking routes in Vic are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 130 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, from rolling hills to forested sections, and the opportunities for both challenging climbs and flowing descents.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or attractions near the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the region around Vic is rich in historical sites. You can find several castles, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3792648" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Can Boix Castle&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4709368" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castell d'Olost&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5079539" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Tona&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, there are religious buildings like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3017238" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de Sant Sebastià&lt;/a&gt;, which is featured on some routes.

Q: Can I find loop trails for mountain biking in Vic?
A: Yes, many of the trails around Vic are designed as loops, offering a complete riding experience without retracing your path. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5334339" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Creu de la Miranda – Sant Quirze de Muntanyola loop from Vic&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23934634" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Pont d'en Bruguer – Salto de la Miñona viewpoint loop from Vic&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the longest mountain bike trail available in Vic?
A: One of the longer routes is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5334339" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Creu de la Miranda – Sant Quirze de Muntanyola loop from Vic&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 29 miles (46.6 km) and features significant elevation changes, making it a challenging ride.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners?
A: Yes, Vic offers 15 easy mountain bike trails perfect for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride. These routes typically have less challenging terrain and elevation changes.

Q: What kind of elevation changes can I expect on the mountain bike trails?
A: The trails around Vic feature varied elevation changes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e961475309/vic-gurb-santa-eulalia-de-riuprimer-la-guixa-vic-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vic – Gurb - Santa Eulàlia de Riuprimer – La Guixa – Vic Route&lt;/a&gt; has an elevation gain of over 215 meters, while more difficult trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3983480" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Creu de la Miranda – Ermita de Sant Sebastià loop from Vic&lt;/a&gt; can have over 600 meters of ascent.
tips: Gurb Enchanted Forest is a very interesting place for children. There are many wooden sculptures. There are also many trails for mountain biking and walking in this area.
Beautiful paths between fields for BTT.
Remains of the church of Sant Martí de Sentfores
This trail is part of the Osona BTT series of trails.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Vic – Gurb - Santa Eulàlia de Riuprimer – La Guixa – Vic Route?
faqs.answer: This mountain biking route is rated as intermediate difficulty, often designated as a 'blue' route within the Vic Plain MTB Centre. It requires good fitness and advanced riding skills, with some sections potentially requiring you to push your bike.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: You can expect to complete this route in approximately 1 hour 30 minutes to 2 hours, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start, and what are the parking options?
faqs.answer: The route officially starts and ends at Alberg Canonge Collell in Vic, which serves as the reception point for the MTB Centre. This location offers amenities like bike rentals and repair facilities. For parking, you'll find options around the Alberg Canonge Collell in Vic. Parking is also available in the towns along the route, such as Gurb, Santa Eulàlia de Riuprimer, and La Guixa, though specific designated MTB parking might be limited outside of Vic.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers varied terrain, combining elements of different trails in the Vic Plain. You'll encounter country roads, pass through towns like Gurb, Santa Eulàlia de Riuprimer, and La Guixa, and ride through fields and forests. While generally 'flowy,' some parts may include rocky sections and small drops, typical of intermediate mountain biking trails.

faqs.question: What points of interest or landmarks will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll pass through the town of Gurb, seeing country houses and the church of Sant Andreu de Gurb, with the castle hill and cross of Gurb in the background. Other highlights include the Enchanted Forest of Gurb, the Path from Villa to Santa Eulalia, the Ruins of the Church of Sant Martí de Sentfores, and sections of the Anilla Verde de Vic.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs on this exact route are not detailed, mountain biking trails in the Osona region generally allow dogs. It's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through towns, near livestock, or in areas with other trail users, to ensure safety and respect for the environment.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: The Vic Plain offers pleasant riding conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are generally ideal, with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be hot, so starting early in the morning is recommended. Winter riding is possible, but be mindful of colder temperatures and potential wet conditions.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other mountain biking trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is part of the extensive Vic Plain MTB Centre network. It incorporates sections of Route 1 (Vic – Gurb – Vic) and Route 2 (Vic – Santa Eulàlia de Riuprimer – La Guixa – Vic). It also intersects with other official trails such as Osona BTT 4, L'Anella Verda de Vic, and the Via cicloturística InterCatalunya, offering opportunities to extend your ride.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to use this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to ride this mountain bike route. It is part of a public network of trails within the Vic Plain MTB Centre.

faqs.question: Are there amenities like cafes or accommodation along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through several towns including Vic, Gurb, Santa Eulàlia de Riuprimer, and La Guixa, you'll find various amenities. Vic, as the starting point, offers numerous cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options. The smaller towns along the way will also have local establishments where you can stop for refreshments or a meal.

faqs.question: Is the route suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its intermediate difficulty rating and requirement for good fitness and advanced riding skills, this specific route might be challenging for younger children or beginner family riders. However, the Vic Plain MTB Centre offers a range of routes, so you might find other, easier options within the network that are more suitable for a family outing.
